sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root בָּצַר No. 2), [pl. ־ִ ים, once -ֹות Dan 11:15 ].

(1) defence (of a city), fortress, Isa 25:12 hence used for a fortified city, i.q. עִיר בְּצוּרָה Isa 17:3 Hab 1:10. The same is עִיר מִבְצָר, e.g. עִיר מִבְצַר עֹר Jos 19:29, and מִבֶעַר עֹר 2Sa 24:7, “defenced Tyre,” i.e. Palætyrus (compare my Comment on Isa 23:7). Pl. עָרֵי מִבְעָר Num 32:36 Jos 19:35 also with both words in pl. עָרֵי מִבְעָרֶיךָ Jer 5:17, and with one only so put עִיר מִבְעָרֹות “defenced cities,” Dan 11:15. (Comp. Hebr. Gram. § 106, 3.)-Metaph. Jer 6:27, “I have set thee as a touchstone amongst my people, מִבְעָר like a defenced fortress,” which is safe from the violence of foes; compare Jer 1:18.

(2) [Mibzar], pr.n. of a prince of the Edomites, Gen 36:42.
